title: Project Scaffold & Database Schema Setup
summary: Initialize Next.js App Router project, install Drizzle ORM, Neon Postgres client, and shadcn/ui. Define the database schema for `gigs` and `messages` tables with the deterministic table prefix (`zerwane_`).
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js project is successfully initialized with Tailwind CSS.
Drizzle ORM is installed and connected to Neon Postgres via `DATABASE_URI`.
Schema definitions for `gigs` and `messages` exist in `db/schema/`.
Database tables use the prefix logic via `db/utils.ts` and `DATABASE_TABLE_PREFIX` environment variable.

title: Global Styling & Layout Components
summary: Configure Tailwind CSS with a heavy metal-inspired dark theme (blacks, high contrast, aggressive typography). Implement the `Header` (with smooth scrolling links) and `Footer` layout components.
acceptanceCriteria: Tailwind configuration defines the heavy metal color palette and typography.
`app/layout.tsx` applies the global dark theme.
A sticky `Header` component exists with navigation links (Hero, About, Gallery, Media, Gigs, Contact) that support smooth scrolling.
A `Footer` component exists with placeholder social media links.

title: Static Sections Implementation
summary: Build the `Hero`, `About`, `Gallery`, and `Media` components using placeholder imagery, aggressive styling, and YouTube/Spotify iframe embeds.
acceptanceCriteria: `Hero` component displays the band name ('Zerwane Struny') and a tagline over an atmospheric placeholder background.
`About` component displays band history and identity.
`Gallery` component renders a responsive CSS grid of live performance placeholder photos.
`Media` component successfully embeds functional Spotify and YouTube iframes.
All sections are responsive on mobile and desktop.

title: Upcoming Gigs Dynamic Section
summary: Implement the `Gigs` Server Component. Fetch upcoming concerts from the `gigs` table via Drizzle ORM and render them. Add a seed script to populate test data.
acceptanceCriteria: A Drizzle query fetches records from the `gigs` table.
The `Gigs` component successfully renders the list of concerts.
A small seed script or utility is available to insert dummy gigs for testing.
Component displays correctly on all screen sizes.

title: Contact Form & Server Action
summary: Implement the `Contact` section with a user-facing form. Create a Next.js Server Action to validate input and securely insert the message into the `messages` table.
acceptanceCriteria: A responsive contact form exists with Name, Email, and Message fields.
Form submission triggers a React Server Action.
The Server Action inserts a new record into the `messages` database table using Drizzle.
Success and error feedback states are handled via React `useActionState` (or similar standard hook).

title: Final Assembly & Deployment Wiring
summary: Assemble all sections into `app/page.tsx`. Verify heavy metal aesthetics, mobile responsiveness, and smooth scrolling. Ensure deployment configuration for Vercel is complete.
acceptanceCriteria: `app/page.tsx` sequentially renders `Hero`, `About`, `Gallery`, `Media`, `Gigs`, and `Contact`.
Smooth scrolling between sections functions correctly.
No horizontal overflow exists on mobile devices.
`package.json` scripts (`build`, `lint`) execute without errors.
Environment variables (`DATABASE_URI`, `DATABASE_TABLE_PREFIX`) are correctly documented for Vercel deployment.
